steps.expected: gguf.GGUFReader opens file with fields and tensors
steps.observed: PASSED - reader loaded with fields > 0 and tensors > 0

steps.expected: Export from PyTorch checkpoint produces valid GGUF file
steps.observed: PASSED - file created, magic verified, tensors present

evidence.description: All 3 GGUF validity tests passed. Output file has correct magic number (0x46554747), is readable by the gguf library, and export from checkpoint produces a valid GGUF with tensors.

steps.expected: FFN TQ1_0 tensors dequantize to values in {-1, 0, 1}
steps.observed: PASSED - all FFN TQ1_0 tensors have ternary values after dequantization

steps.expected: Every layer's FFN gate, up, down are TQ1_0 quantized
steps.observed: PASSED - 6 FFN tensors found across 2 layers, all TQ1_0
PASSED - round-trip produces only ternary values

evidence.description: All 3 ternary fidelity tests passed. TQ1_0 quantized FFN weights dequantize to strictly ternary values {-1, 0, 1}. All FFN layers have TQ1_0 quantization. Round-trip quantization-dequantization preserves ternary values.

assertions.title: GGUF file metadata present

evidence.description: All 5 metadata tests passed (plus 2 bonus tests). All four required metadata keys present with correct values: general.name, bitnet.context_length, bitnet.embedding_length, bitnet.vocab_size. All values are non-zero/non-empty.

assertions.testOutput:
TestGGUFMetadata::test_model_name_present PASSED
TestGGUFMetadata::test_context_length_present PASSED
TestGGUFMetadata::test_embedding_dim_present PASSED
TestGGUFMetadata::test_vocab_size_present PASSED
TestGGUFMetadata::test_metadata_values_are_nonzero PASSED
TestGGUFMetadata::test_all_required_metadata_from_export PASSED
TestGGUFMetadata::test_architecture_metadata PASSED

summary: Tested 3 assertions: all 3 passed. Full test suite: 32 passed, 0 failed, 2 warnings (DeprecationWarnings from SwigPyPacked/SwigPyObject, unrelated to test logic). VAL-EXPORT-001: GGUF file validity confirmed (magic number, library readability, checkpoint export). VAL-EXPORT-002: Ternary weight fidelity confirmed (TQ1_0 dequantization, all layers, round-trip). VAL-EXPORT-003: All required metadata present with non-zero values.
